title: A judge, referee or similar official doesn’t need a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## To work in Canada as a judge, referee or similar official
FIFA-invited workers and volunteers
You don’t need a work permit to work in Canada if you’ve been directly invited to work at the FIFA World Cup 26™.
You may need to apply for a visitor visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) .
You’re an official at an international amateur competition who will judge or be an official for an artistic or cultural event, such as
- a music and dance festival
- an agricultural contest
If this describes you, you don’t need a work permit.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
